metadata.summary: On the night of October 15, 2023, the body of Arjun Mehta, a 48-year-old real estate developer, was found in the backyard of his bungalow in Kharadi, Pune, with a single stab wound to the chest and signs of a struggle. The crime scene showed no forced entry, suggesting the killer was known to him. A bloodied kitchen knife, registered under Arjun’s name, was found nearby, but fingerprints were partially wiped. The initial FIR filed by his wife, Priya Mehta, claimed she heard a scream at 10:30 PM and found him dead upon rushing out. However, CCTV footage from a neighbor’s gate shows Priya leaving the house at 9:45 PM and returning at 11:15 PM — contradicting her statement. Arjun had recently finalized the sale of a disputed plot of land to his business partner, Vikram Desai, after a bitter legal battle with his estranged brother, Rohan Mehta, who claimed ancestral rights. Financial records show a sudden ₹25 lakh transfer from Arjun’s account to an unknown beneficiary the day before his death. The autopsy placed time of death between 9:30 PM and 10:15 PM, but a neighbor claims hearing a loud argument at 10:45 PM. Forensic analysis revealed trace DNA under Arjun’s fingernails — not matching any immediate family — and a partial palm print on the backdoor handle that doesn’t match any registered occupants. The case is further complicated by the sudden disappearance of Arjun’s personal assistant, Nidhi Rao, who had access to his phone and financial apps. Witnesses include household staff, neighbors, business associates, and a local priest who claims to have seen Arjun arguing with a hooded figure near the temple gate at 8:45 PM — a claim later retracted under pressure. The investigation has uncovered evidence of bribed testimony, forged property documents, and a hidden extramarital affair with a yoga instructor who denies any contact after September.

true_timeline: 2023-10-15 08:45 PM: Vikram meets Arjun at the Shree Ganesh Temple gate to demand final signature on forged property transfer documents; Arjun refuses.
2023-10-15 09:15 PM: Vikram returns to Arjun’s house via service entrance (key obtained from Nidhi Rao); they argue in the kitchen.
2023-10-15 09:30 PM: Vikram stabs Arjun during the argument; Arjun scratches Vikram’s left forearm during struggle.
2023-10-15 09:35 PM: Vikram wipes knife, plants it near the garden, and exits through the backdoor, leaving partial palm print.
2023-10-15 09:40 PM: Nidhi Rao, acting on Vikram’s instructions, transfers ₹25 lakh from Arjun’s account to a shell account under 'R. Sharma'.
2023-10-15 09:45 PM: Priya Mehta leaves house to meet her yoga instructor, claiming she’s attending a meditation retreat.
2023-10-15 10:10 PM: Vikram calls Nidhi Rao to confirm transfer success; she deletes call logs.
2023-10-15 10:15 PM: Arjun dies from internal bleeding; body undiscovered.
2023-10-15 11:15 PM: Priya returns home, stages discovery, calls police.
2023-10-16: Nidhi Rao flees to Goa; Vikram bribes two neighbors to claim they heard an argument at 10:45 PM.
forged_evidence: Forged property deed signed by Arjun (dated 2023-10-14) with altered thumbprint, submitted by Vikram to register land transfer
Fake WhatsApp message screenshot showing Arjun threatening Vikram, created by Nidhi Rao using Arjun’s phone backup
Staged blood spatter pattern on kitchen floor to imply struggle occurred near the sink, when actual struggle was near the backdoor

injected_contradictions.description: Priya claims she heard a scream at 10:30 PM, but CCTV shows her returning at 11:15 PM and the autopsy says death occurred by 10:15 PM

Father Thomas initially claims he saw Arjun arguing with a hooded man at 8:45 PM, but later retracts, saying he was mistaken — yet his original statement was recorded by a police constable who later admits he was paid ₹50,000 to 'clarify' it
Forensic report says DNA under Arjun’s nails matches no one in the database, but it actually matches Vikram — whose DNA was never entered because he never had a criminal record
Nidhi Rao’s phone shows no calls to Vikram after September, but call logs were wiped and a backup from a cloud account reveals 17 calls in the last week
The knife has Arjun’s fingerprints on the handle, but none on the blade — suggesting it was planted after the murder, yet the family claims he always kept it in the kitchen drawer

A neighbor claims to have seen a white sedan (Vikram’s car) leaving at 10:20 PM, but the car’s GPS shows it was parked at Vikram’s office until 11:00 PM — the GPS data was spoofed using a device bought online

contradictions.claim_a: Priya Mehta claims she heard a scream at 10:30 PM and discovered the body immediately.
contradictions.claim_b: Autopsy report concludes death occurred between 9:30 PM and 10:15 PM, and CCTV shows Priya returning home at 11:15 PM.

contradictions.claim_a: Father Thomas states he saw Arjun arguing with a hooded man at 8:45 PM near the temple.
contradictions.claim_b: Father Thomas later retracts, saying he was confused by the lighting, but his original recorded statement was filed by a constable who was later found to have received a cash deposit of ₹50,000.

contradictions.claim_a: DNA under Arjun’s fingernails does not match any registered individual in the police database.
contradictions.claim_b: The DNA profile matches Vikram Desai, whose DNA was never entered due to lack of prior criminal record — but it was collected during a voluntary blood donation in 2021.

contradictions.claim_a: Arjun’s bank statement shows a ₹25 lakh transfer to 'R. Sharma' on October 15 at 9:40 PM, labeled 'personal loan'.
contradictions.claim_b: Nidhi Rao claims she never accessed Arjun’s phone after 9 PM, but digital forensics show the transfer was made via his phone’s biometric login, which only he and Nidhi knew.

contradictions.claim_a: Mrs. Kulkarni (Neighbor) claims she heard a loud argument at 10:45 PM coming from Arjun’s house.
contradictions.claim_b: Her home security camera footage shows her own porch light was off at 10:45 PM, making it impossible to see or hear clearly — she later admits she was paid to 'confirm' the time.

contradictions.claim_a: The murder weapon has Arjun’s fingerprints on the handle and no prints on the blade.
contradictions.claim_b: Blood spatter analysis shows the knife was wiped after use, but the blade shows no trace of Arjun’s blood — suggesting it was not the weapon used, yet it was planted at the scene.

contradictions.claim_a: Vikram Desai claims he was at his office until 11:00 PM and has CCTV footage to prove it.
contradictions.claim_b: The office CCTV footage shows his car parked outside, but the timestamp on the footage was altered — the original footage shows his car leaving at 9:50 PM.

contradictions.claim_a: Nidhi Rao’s phone shows no communication with Vikram after September 2023.
contradictions.claim_b: A cloud backup of her phone reveals 17 encrypted WhatsApp messages with Vikram between October 10–15, deleted from her device but recoverable via forensic imaging.

contradictions.claim_a: Rohan Mehta claims the disputed land was ancestral property and Arjun had no right to sell it.
contradictions.claim_b: The property deed presented by Vikram shows Arjun as sole owner since 2018, but the original 2005 deed (found in Arjun’s safe) names Rohan as co-owner — the 2018 deed was forged.

contradictions.claim_a: Priya Mehta claims she had no knowledge of Arjun’s financial affairs and was financially dependent on him.
contradictions.claim_b: Bank records show Priya opened a new savings account in her name on October 14 with a ₹10 lakh deposit — the same day Vikram transferred funds to Nidhi Rao’s account.

documents.content: I, Priya Mehta, wife of the deceased Arjun Mehta, residing at Bungalow No. 17, Sector 22, Kharadi, Pune, hereby file this FIR regarding the murder of my husband. On the night of 15th October 2023, at approximately 10:30 PM, I heard a loud scream coming from the backyard of our residence. I immediately rushed out and found my husband lying on the ground with a deep stab wound to his chest. A bloodied kitchen knife was found nearby. There were signs of a struggle — overturned chairs, broken glass, and blood spatter near the kitchen sink. I called 100 immediately. My husband had no enemies, but recently had a bitter dispute over property with his brother Rohan Mehta. I have no knowledge of anyone else being present that night. I have not left the house since returning from evening yoga at 8:00 PM. I am in shock and request immediate investigation. Signed, Priya Mehta.

I have been married to Arjun for 22 years. We had a quiet life. He was stressed lately because of the land dispute with his brother, but he never spoke of violence. On the night of the incident, I returned home from my meditation retreat at 8:00 PM. I prepared dinner, watched TV, and went to bed around 9:15 PM. I woke up at 10:30 PM to a scream. I ran out and found him dead. I did not leave the house after 8 PM. I have no idea who could have done this. The knife is ours — he always kept it in the kitchen drawer. I don’t know anything about the ₹25 lakh transfer. I swear on my children’s lives that I did not see anyone else that night.

I have known Arjun for 15 years. We were finalizing the sale of the disputed plot in Kharadi. The deal was settled on October 14th. I was at my office from 9 AM until 11 PM on the 15th. My security cameras show my car parked outside. I received a call from Nidhi Rao at 10:10 PM asking if the transfer was complete — I told her yes. I had no reason to kill Arjun. The land was legally mine. I never met him after 5 PM that day. I don’t know why someone would frame me. I have no idea about the DNA or the knife. I have never been to his house except for business meetings.

Arjun stole our ancestral land. The original deed from 2005 clearly names me as co-owner. He forged documents in 2018 to remove my name. I confronted him at the temple gate on October 15th around 8:30 PM — I saw him arguing with a man in a white coat, but I didn’t recognize him. I left because I didn’t want to escalate. I didn’t kill him. I was home alone all night. I didn’t know about the money transfer. I have no access to his phone or bank account. I only want my share of the property back. The police are ignoring my documents. Why is Vikram’s forged deed being accepted?

On the evening of October 15th, I was walking home from the temple after evening prayers. Around 8:45 PM, I saw Arjun Mehta standing near the temple gate, arguing loudly with a tall man wearing a hoodie. The man had a briefcase. I thought it was a property dispute — I didn’t get close. I didn’t see the man’s face. I told the police this in my initial statement. But later, I realized the lighting was poor and I may have confused him with someone else. I am sorry — I retract my statement. I was not sure. I didn’t see anything else.

I heard a loud argument coming from Arjun’s house at 10:45 PM. It sounded like two men shouting — one was Arjun, the other deeper. I looked out my window but it was dark. I didn’t see anyone. I told the police because I thought it was important. I didn’t know Arjun was dead then. I didn’t see a car leave. I just heard noise. I don’t know who it was. I swear I’m telling the truth.

I worked for Arjun for 4 years. I handled his phone, calendar, and financial apps. On October 15th, I left work at 6 PM. I was at home all night. I did not access his phone after 9 PM. I did not make any transfers. I have no idea about the ₹25 lakh. I don’t know who killed him. I didn’t talk to Vikram in October. My phone was lost last week — maybe someone stole it. I was scared after the murder, so I went to Goa to visit my sister. I didn’t run away. I just needed space.

I was assigned to record Father Thomas’s initial statement on October 16th. He clearly said he saw Arjun arguing with a hooded man at 8:45 PM. I typed it verbatim. Later, on October 18th, he came to the station and asked me to change his statement. He said he was mistaken. I told him I could not alter official records. He then handed me an envelope with ₹50,000 in cash and said, 'For the family.' I refused. I did not accept it. But I am afraid. I am not sure what to do now. I did not alter the original statement — it is still in the evidence locker.

I work for the Mehtas since 2019. On the night of the murder, I left at 8:30 PM. I saw Priya Ma’am leave at 9:45 PM in her SUV. She said she was going to a friend’s house. I didn’t see anyone else come or go. I heard no screams. I cleaned the kitchen before leaving. The knife was in the drawer as always. I don’t know about the money or the land. I am just a worker. I don’t want trouble.

I knew Arjun through yoga classes. We had an affair for 8 months — ended in September. I had no contact with him after September 25th. I have no idea about his death. I was in Rishikesh on October 15th. My retreat center has attendance logs. I have never met Vikram or Nidhi. I don’t know why anyone would link me to this. I am not hiding anything. My phone records prove I was not in Pune that night.

metadata.reviewed_by: Digital Forensics Unit, Pune Cyber Cell

documents.content: CCTV footage from House No. 19 shows the following: - 09:45 PM: Priya Mehta exits her bungalow in a white SUV (registration MH-12-AB-7890). - 11:15 PM: Priya Mehta returns to her bungalow. - 09:50 PM: A white sedan (Vikram Desai’s car, MH-12-CD-4567) is seen entering the service lane behind Arjun’s bungalow, but the camera angle does not capture the entrance. - 10:20 PM: The same sedan is seen leaving the service lane. - 10:45 PM: No vehicles are observed near Arjun’s property. Footage timestamps were verified against NTP server logs. No evidence of tampering found. Note: The sedan’s license plate was partially obscured in the 10:20 PM clip, but vehicle model and color match Vikram Desai’s registered vehicle.

metadata.examined_by: Dr. N. Joshi, Senior Forensic Pathologist

documents.content: Autopsy of Arjun Mehta (Age 48) conducted on October 16, 2023. Cause of death: Single penetrating stab wound to the left chest, severing the left ventricle. Time of death: Between 9:30 PM and 10:15 PM, based on body temperature, rigor mortis, and vitreous humor potassium levels. No signs of sexual assault or poisoning. Defensive wounds: Abrasions and lacerations on right palm and left forearm. Trace DNA under fingernails: Profile matches no database entry. Blood spatter analysis: Pattern inconsistent with struggle near kitchen sink — most spatter concentrated near backdoor. Murder weapon: Kitchen knife (registered to victim). Fingerprints on handle: Match Arjun Mehta. No fingerprints on blade. Blood on blade: None detected. Trace fibers on blade: Synthetic polyester, not from victim’s clothing. Conclusion: Weapon likely wiped post-mortem and planted at scene. DNA under nails is male, non-family, likely perpetrator.

Device recovered from victim’s study. Biometric login (fingerprint) enabled. Forensic imaging reveals: - ₹25 lakh transfer to 'R. Sharma' (Account: IB00123456789) initiated via UPI at 09:40:12 PM on 10/15/2023. Authentication: Fingerprint match. - WhatsApp logs: All messages from 10/10–10/15 deleted. Recovery via iCloud backup reveals 17 encrypted messages between Nidhi Rao and Vikram Desai discussing transfer, alibi, and 'cleanup.' - Fake screenshot of threatening WhatsApp message from Arjun to Vikram created using Arjun’s template and saved as 'land_deal.jpg' in Downloads folder. - Call log: One call to Nidhi Rao at 10:10:03 PM on 10/15 — duration 42 seconds — deleted from device, recovered via carrier backup. - Location history: Victim’s phone was stationary at home from 9:00 PM to 10:15 PM. Nidhi Rao’s phone (Android) was at her residence during same period, but cloud sync shows location spoofing via VPN app installed on October 14th.

metadata.bank: State Bank of India, Kharadi Branch

documents.content: Transaction Summary: - 10/14/2023: ₹10,00,000 transferred from Arjun Mehta’s account to Priya Mehta’s newly opened savings account (A/C No: SBIN00987654321). - 10/15/2023: ₹25,00,000 transferred to 'R. Sharma' (Account: IB00123456789) via UPI. Label: 'Personal Loan'. - 10/15/2023: ₹5,00,000 withdrawn in cash from ATM (Kharadi Central). - 10/16/2023: ₹1,00,000 transferred from Vikram Desai’s account to Nidhi Rao’s account (labeled 'consulting fee'). Bank records confirm Nidhi Rao opened account on 10/13/2023. Priya’s new account was opened on 10/14/2023 with ₹10 lakh deposit — matching the transfer from Arjun. No prior deposits in Priya’s account since 2020. R. Sharma account is shell account registered under fake PAN. No physical person identified.

Original 2005 Deed: Names Arjun Mehta and Rohan Mehta as co-owners. Signed by both brothers and registered with Sub-Registrar, Pune. 2018 Deed: Presented by Vikram Desai to register transfer. Names Arjun Mehta as sole owner. Signed by Arjun Mehta. Thumbprint on signature: Digitally altered. Comparison with 2005 deed shows mismatched ridge patterns. Forensic analysis confirms 2018 deed was printed on paper manufactured after 2017, but the ink and signature were scanned from a 2005 document and digitally superimposed. The 2005 deed was found hidden in Arjun’s safe behind a false panel. Vikram’s legal team submitted the forged 2018 deed to register land transfer on October 14, 2023 — approved by Sub-Registrar on October 16, 2023, before fraud was detected.

Summary: Victim Arjun Mehta was murdered between 9:30 PM and 10:15 PM on October 15, 2023, at his residence. No forced entry. Weapon: Kitchen knife, planted post-mortem. DNA under victim’s nails matches no database, but matches Vikram Desai’s 2021 blood donation record (collected at Pune Blood Bank). Nidhi Rao’s digital footprint confirms communication with Vikram Desai and execution of ₹25 lakh transfer. Priya Mehta’s alibi is false — CCTV shows her leaving at 9:45 PM. Mrs. Kulkarni’s testimony about hearing argument at 10:45 PM is false — her porch light was off and her camera was disabled at that time. Father Thomas’s initial statement was recorded, but later retracted after cash payment from unknown source (Constable Ramesh has reported bribe attempt). Vikram Desai’s office CCTV was spoofed — original footage shows his car leaving at 9:50 PM. The forged property deed was created by Vikram using Nidhi Rao’s access to Arjun’s documents. Nidhi Rao fled to Goa on October 16. Motive: Financial gain through property fraud and elimination of victim. Suspects: Vikram Desai (primary), Nidhi Rao (accomplice). Priya Mehta may be complicit in financial fraud. Case forwarded for charge sheet.

IN THE COURT OF THE SESSIONS JUDGE, PUNE

Charge Sheet under Sections 302, 120B, 420, 467, 468, 471, and 201 IPC

Accused: Vikram Desai and Nidhi Rao

The accused Vikram Desai, along with Nidhi Rao, conspired to eliminate Arjun Mehta to gain control of disputed property and illegally transfer ₹25 lakh. On October 15, 2023, Vikram Desai entered Arjun Mehta’s residence via service entrance using a key obtained from Nidhi Rao. An argument ensued, during which Vikram stabbed Arjun. Nidhi Rao, acting under Vikram’s direction, transferred ₹25 lakh from Arjun’s account. The murder weapon was planted, and false testimony was orchestrated through bribed witnesses. Forged property documents were submitted to register land transfer. Nidhi Rao fled after the crime. Priya Mehta is under investigation for financial conspiracy. Evidence includes DNA match, digital communications, financial trail, and CCTV anomalies. Charge sheet submitted for trial.

JUDGE: Mr. Desai, you claim you were at your office until 11 PM. The original CCTV footage shows you leaving at 9:50 PM. How do you explain this?

Vikram Desai: Your Honor, I have a witness — my security guard. The footage must be wrong. My car was parked outside the whole time.

PROSECUTOR: Your Honor, the footage was tampered with. The timestamp was altered. We have the original file from the CCTV server.

JUDGE: And the DNA under the victim’s nails — matches you, Mr. Desai. You never had a criminal record, so your DNA was never entered. But you donated blood in 2021.

Vikram Desai: That’s true. I donated blood after my mother’s surgery. But I didn’t kill him.

JUDGE: The court notes the contradiction between your alibi and forensic evidence. Bail denied. Next hearing: December 10, 2023.

queries.question: What is the true timeline of the murder?
Which witness statements are intentionally deceptive and why?
How was the ₹25 lakh transfer executed, and who had the means to do it?
Which piece of forensic evidence most directly implicates the perpetrator?
What is the nature of the forged property document, and how was it created?
How were digital communications manipulated to mislead investigators?
What is the relationship between Nidhi Rao and Vikram Desai, and how did it contribute to the crime?
Why did Priya Mehta lie about her whereabouts, and what was her financial motive?
Based on the evidence, what is the likelihood of conviction for Vikram Desai and Nidhi Rao?

expected_answers.answer: The actual perpetrator is Vikram Desai. He entered Arjun’s home using a key obtained from Nidhi Rao, argued with Arjun over the forged property deed, stabbed him during the struggle, and planted the knife. His DNA was found under Arjun’s fingernails during the fight, and his car was seen entering the service lane at 9:50 PM — contradicting his alibi. He orchestrated the transfer of ₹25 lakh, bribed witnesses, and forged documents to secure the property. His motive was financial gain through the fraudulent land sale.

The true timeline is: 8:45 PM — Vikram meets Arjun at temple gate demanding signature on forged deed; Arjun refuses. 9:15 PM — Vikram enters house via service entrance using key from Nidhi. 9:30 PM — Argument escalates; Vikram stabs Arjun. Arjun scratches Vikram’s forearm. 9:35 PM — Vikram wipes knife, plants it in garden, exits via backdoor leaving partial palm print. 9:40 PM — Nidhi Rao transfers ₹25 lakh using Arjun’s biometric login. 9:45 PM — Priya leaves to meet her yoga instructor. 10:10 PM — Vikram calls Nidhi to confirm transfer. 10:15 PM — Arjun dies. 11:15 PM — Priya returns and stages discovery. Nidhi flees on 10/16. Neighbors bribed to claim argument at 10:45 PM.

Priya Mehta is deceptive — she claims she heard a scream at 10:30 PM and discovered the body immediately, but CCTV shows her leaving at 9:45 PM and returning at 11:15 PM, and autopsy confirms death occurred by 10:15 PM. Mrs. Kulkarni falsely claims to have heard an argument at 10:45 PM; her porch light was off and she admitted to being paid. Father Thomas initially identified a hooded figure at 8:45 PM, but retracted after receiving ₹50,000 from an unknown source (linked to Vikram via Constable Ramesh). All three are lying to cover up the real timeline and protect the actual perpetrator.

The ₹25 lakh transfer was executed via UPI using Arjun’s smartphone’s biometric login at 9:40 PM. Only Arjun and Nidhi Rao had access to his fingerprint authentication. Nidhi Rao, acting under Vikram’s instructions, performed the transfer. Digital forensics confirm the transaction was initiated from Arjun’s phone, which was in his study at the time. Nidhi had previously installed a remote access tool on the phone. The money was sent to a shell account under 'R. Sharma,' which is untraceable. This confirms Nidhi’s direct involvement and Vikram’s control over the scheme.

The DNA under Arjun’s fingernails is the most directly incriminating forensic evidence. It matches Vikram Desai’s 2021 blood donation profile — a match not in police databases because he had no criminal record. This proves physical contact during a struggle, placing Vikram at the scene at the time of death. The DNA was not planted, and the victim’s defensive wounds confirm the struggle occurred during the attack. No other suspect has a biological link to this evidence.

The forged property deed was a digitally altered version of the original 2005 deed. The 2018 version, submitted by Vikram, falsely listed Arjun as sole owner. Forensic analysis showed the signature was scanned from the original 2005 document and superimposed onto a newer paper stock. The thumbprint was digitally altered using software to match Arjun’s prints. The original 2005 deed was hidden in Arjun’s safe, proving the forgery. Nidhi Rao had access to scanned copies of Arjun’s documents and likely assisted in creating the forgery using her administrative role.

Digital communications were manipulated in three ways: (1) Nidhi Rao deleted 17 encrypted WhatsApp messages with Vikram from her device, but they were recovered from iCloud backup. (2) A fake WhatsApp screenshot showing Arjun threatening Vikram was created using Arjun’s message template and saved as 'land_deal.jpg' to mislead investigators. (3) Nidhi’s phone showed no recent calls to Vikram, but call logs were wiped using a cleaning app; carrier backups revealed 17 calls between Oct 10–15. These manipulations were designed to create a false narrative of no collusion.

Nidhi Rao and Vikram Desai were co-conspirators. Nidhi, as Arjun’s assistant, had full access to his phone, documents, and financial apps. Vikram bribed her to obtain access to his biometric login and confidential files. She transferred the ₹25 lakh and helped create the forged deed. Their relationship was professional but criminal — they communicated via encrypted messages and coordinated the murder and cover-up. She fled to Goa after the crime, indicating premeditated escape. He provided the motive and direction; she provided the access and execution.

Priya Mehta lied about her whereabouts to create an alibi. She left at 9:45 PM to meet her yoga instructor, whom she was still seeing despite claiming the affair ended in September. She returned at 11:15 PM and staged the discovery to appear innocent. Her financial motive was revealed when ₹10 lakh was transferred to her new account on October 14 — the same day Vikram transferred ₹5 lakh to Nidhi. She likely knew about the scheme and benefited financially, making her an accomplice in the fraud, even if not directly involved in the murder.

The likelihood of conviction for Vikram Desai and Nidhi Rao is very high. The evidence is overwhelming: DNA match, digital communications proving conspiracy, financial trail, forged documents, CCTV anomalies, witness bribes, and the recovered original deed. Priya’s involvement is less certain but likely for fraud. The court has already denied bail to Vikram based on forensic and digital evidence. With Nidhi’s extradition from Goa and the confession of Constable Ramesh regarding bribery, the prosecution’s case is solid. Conviction under Sections 302 and 120B IPC is highly probable.
